subject: BLOCKERS: WS1.4 no-I/O and domain-boundary assumptions
body:
Coordinator directive from Luminous Threading Asteroid.

Ramanujan/assumptions-expert returned additional BLOCKERS for WS1.4. Please fix before any staging/commit:

1. graph-core no-I/O invariant: jsonld.js defaults can dereference remote contexts in Node without a constrained documentLoader. The adapter/driver interface must prevent remote dereferencing or otherwise make no-I/O explicit and tested. Inline-context tests are not enough.

2. Oak Threads framing test leaks consumer/domain semantics into graph-core and overclaims a pinned raw import. Move/remove Oak-specific curric:Thread/includesThread semantics from graph-core tests; keep substrate tests generic, or move domain proof to graph-corpus-sdk in a later owned lane.

3. Comment: narrow wording around the adapter seam to jsonld.js v9 adapter unless the implementation discriminator is genuinely replaceable.

Rawls/test blocker on test classification also still stands. Hubble/type review is pending. Hold the commit window, apply fixes under your existing claim, rerun graph-core test/type-check/lint/build and git diff --check, then report updated readiness with how each blocker was addressed.
